content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation for Mitigating Social Bias in Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models often exhibit social biases and stereotypes in their outputs, even when given ostensibly neutral prompts. Current prompting strategies are insufficient to systematically reduce such biases without extensive model retraining.",
  "motivation": "De-biasing LLM outputs is crucial for trustworthy and fair deployment in sensitive applications. As LLMs are increasingly used in real-world settings, mitigating social bias at the prompt level provides an accessible, model-agnostic intervention. Prompt-based solutions can be rapidly iterated and deployed without access to model internals.",
  "method": "We propose Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation (CPA): for any given user prompt, automatically generate and prepend a set of counterfactual sub-prompts that explicitly negate or swap demographic attributes (e.g., gender, race, age) relevant to the context. The model generates outputs for both the original and counterfactual prompts, and then a prompt-level ensembling mechanism selects or synthesizes a final response that minimizes divergence and stereotypes across demographic variants. The CPA module is model-agnostic and can be integrated into any LLM interface.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CPA on standard bias benchmarks such as StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs, and RealToxicityPrompts. Baselines include vanilla prompting, instruction-based de-biasing, and self-debiasing prompts. Metrics are bias scores (e.g., SS and ICAT), toxicity rates, and human evaluations of stereotypical content. Ablations will test the contribution of different counterfactual generation strategies and ensembling mechanisms."
}
